Tests the behavior of the modulus operation with NaN values in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that uses the `assert` function to verify the behavior of the modulus operator with `NaN` (Not-a-Number) values in JavaScript. It checks that the result of the expression `NaN % NaN` is `NaN`, which is consistent with the IEEE 754 standard for floating-point arithmetic. The use of `assert` ensures that the test will fail if the expression does not evaluate to `NaN`, indicating a deviation from expected behavior. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on validating the behavior of the modulus operation with `NaN`.
